title: agrimonia eupatoria var. incisa
intro: Agrimonia eupatoria var. incisa, also known as cut-leaf agrimony, is a variant of the Agrimonia eupatoria plant, which is a flowering plant in the rose family that is native to Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Agrimonia eupatoria var. incisa include its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and act as an antimicrobial agent, making it a popular remedy for various ailments. Agrimonia eupatoria var. incisa can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
